response.justification: Bogdan is a credible Staff-level IC with a long polyglot track record and, critically, is already hands-on with AI-agent infrastructure and MCP framework development at re:cinq — satisfying the AI-native baseline the role treats as table-stakes. His Dunnhumby tenure demonstrates genuine high-throughput distributed-systems experience at scale (millions of HTTP requests per second). However, Python — listed as a must-have — is absent from both his skills section and every role description, which is a non-trivial gap. Operational ownership signals (on-call, incident response) are also missing from the resume, and his most relevant infrastructure work is now roughly six years old, with recent roles skewing toward product-layer TypeScript delivery.
strengths: Currently building AI-agent infrastructure and MCP frameworks at re:cinq, directly satisfying the AI-native engineering baseline the role requires
Demonstrated high-throughput distributed-systems work at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency micro-services architecture
Consistent Staff-level IC tenure with ownership signals — founding an open-source Kubernetes-deployable geo-data platform, running workshops, and delivering across the full stack
concerns: Python is a stated must-have yet is entirely absent from the skills list and every role description in the resume
No explicit mention of on-call rotations, incident response, or operational ownership at production scale — a core requirement for this role

The high-throughput distributed-systems depth (Dunnhumby) dates to 2018–2020; the subsequent years have been product-layer TypeScript/Node work, leaving the infrastructure muscle potentially underexercised
